purpose: Implement the Phase 10 P0 damage-timing-rider-adapter contract by adding a pre-ActorEngine.applyDamage packet adapter for hit/damage timing riders.
implementationScope: Collect feat/talent damage timing metadata from abilityMeta.damageTimingRules, damageRiders, damageRules, targetDamageRules, and abilityMeta.rules.
Provide a compatibility rule for Advantageous Attack so it is modeled as a target-not-acted damage rider rather than an attack bonus.
Build a declarative modified damage packet before ActorEngine.applyDamage runs.
Preserve applied rider audit data in damagePacket.options.damageTimingRiders.
Expose targetHasActedThisEncounter helper for future attack/damage context wiring.
nonGoals: No automatic attack roll or chat UI integration in this slice.
No post-resolution target effects; those belong to CombatTargetEffectAdapter or later dedicated handlers.

advantageousAttackHandling.reason: The adapter now has a built-in compatibility rule that adds one-half actor level to damage when the target has not acted yet. This replaces the wrong-shape attack-bonus approach at the adapter layer, but audit promotion should wait until the damage UI/workflow calls the adapter.

unlocksPhase9Families: damage rider audit trail in damagePacket.options
remainingWork: Wire rollDamage or damage-application UI to call DamageTimingRiderAdapter.applyToDamagePacket before ActorEngine.applyDamage.
Normalize individual feat metadata to supported damage timing rule types.
Add exact acted-this-encounter flags on combat turn start/end instead of relying on conservative combat order inference.
Promote Advantageous Attack out of implemented_incorrect only after the wrong-shape metadata is quarantined and the damage workflow calls the adapter.
